question: For the quarterly platform engagement distribution, which interpretation best reflects what can (and cannot) be inferred from the 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: The chart allows comparison of the absolute number of page views between quarters.
options.B: Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking comparisons of the metrics; invalid inferences include cross-quarter comparisons of their absolute quantities.
options.C: User registrations, support tickets, and page views all follow the same trend over the year because the bars are normalized to 100%.
options.D: Q3 has the highest overall platform engagement because its stacked bar appears most balanced across the three metrics.

explanation: 100% stacking normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking (e.g., one metric > another in every quarter). Invalid inferences include cross-quarter total comparisons or combining unrelated categories.
